questions.question: Which institution awards the Ustad Bismillah Khan Yuva Puraskar?
questions.explanation: Ustad Bismillah Khan Yuva Puraskar 2019, 2020 and 2021 have been awarded by the Sangeet Natak Akademi to 102 artists. Sangeet Natak Akademi, the national academy of Music, Dance and Drama, is the apex body of the performing arts in the country. The Ustad Bismillah Khan Yuva Puraskar is given to artists up to the age of 40 years, aims to encourage outstanding young talents in diverse fields of the performing arts.

questions.question: Which city is the host of the first-ever G20 Culture Working Group (CWG) meeting?
questions.explanation: The first-ever G20 Culture Working Group (CWG) meeting is set to take place in Khajuraho, India. It will focus on protecting and restoring cultural property. This exhibition will showcase 25 artifacts that have been returned to India after being smuggled out of the country. The CWG’s goal is to reduce illicit trafficking of cultural property by 2030, regulate online trading platforms, and raise public awareness about restitution laws and conventions.

questions.question: Which country passed a New Citizenship Law that allows deportation and stripping of citizenship?
questions.explanation: Israel has passed a new Citizenship Law that allows the deportation and stripping of citizenship of individuals who received funding from the Palestinian Authority or an associated organization. This legislation amends Israel’s 1952 Citizenship Law. The new law applies to Israeli citizens and permanent residents, who are imprisoned after being convicted for terror, aiding terror, harming sovereignty, inciting war and assisting the adversary during the times of war.

questions.question: ‘DUSTLIK’ is a military training exercise undertaken by India with which country?
questions.explanation: India-Uzbekistan biennial military training Exercise DUSTLIK 2023 will be conducted in Pithoragarh, Uttarakhand. The Indian contingent will be represented by the 14th Battalion of the Garhwal Rifles, part of Western Command in this bilateral exercise. This joint exercise is aimed at enhancing military capability to undertake multi-domain operations in a Sub Conventional scenario under United Nations Mandate.

questions.question: Which country launched the ‘Hwasong-15’ is the Intercontinental ballistic missile?
questions.explanation: The Hwasong-15 intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) has been recently launched at Pyongyang International Airport, in North Korea. South Korea alleged that North Korea could test-fire intercontinental ballistic missiles on a lower, longer trajectory and conduct its seventh nuclear test this year. North Korea has so far conducted ICBM tests only on lofted trajectories.

questions.question: AMASR Act, which was seen in the news, is related to which field?
questions.explanation: The AMASR (Amendment) Bill is a proposed legislation that aims to make several changes to the Ancient Monuments and Archaeological Sites and Remains (AMASR) Act. The Act regulates the preservation of historical monuments and archaeological sites. It seeks to redefine the current definition of a ‘monument’ which currently requires it to be at least 100 years old.

questions.question: Which country recently ratified the country’s entry into the Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership (RCEP)?
questions.explanation: The Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership (RCEP) is a free trade agreement between 15 countries in the Asia-Pacific region. The Philippines Senate recently ratified the country’s entry into the RCEP. The RCEP, which was signed in 2020, represents the largest trade bloc in history. RCEP aims to increase economic integration and reduce trade barriers among its member nations.

questions.question: Rideau Canal Skateway, which was seen in the news, is in which country?
questions.explanation: The Rideau Canal Skateway is a popular winter attraction in Ottawa, Canada. It is the world’s largest skating rink. It is part of the Rideau Canal, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. For the first time since it was built in 1971, Rideau Canal Skateway will not open because of the lack of ice. This is due to the unusually high temperature in the region.

questions.question: Lake Garda, which was seen in the news, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Lake Garda, which is famed for its clear waters, is situated in northern Italy. The historically low waters of Lake Garda caused by a winter drought have led to an unusual phenomenon of a sand and stone causeway appearing to connect a small island to the mainland. The decline in water levels is attributed to the warm temperatures, lack of rain in six weeks, and declining snow in the surrounding mountains.

questions.question: ‘National Health Authority’s Scan and Share Service’ was launched as part of which scheme?
questions.explanation: National Health Authority’s Scan and Share service has been adopted by 365 hospitals across 25 states and UTs in India within five months of its launch. It was launched as part of Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission. The service enables paperless and instant token generation for outpatient department (OPD) registration using a unique QR code displayed by participating hospitals.
